description:
Delete src/analyzer/graph-builder.ts (the legacy @deprecated Map-based DependencyGraph builder) after migrating its remaining internal consumers.

WO-MIGRATE-GRAPH-BUILDER-IMPORTERS-001 cleared all 14 external consumer imports. Deletion was deferred because 9 internal files still import from this module:

Internal src/analyzer/ files (relative imports):
  - src/analyzer/analyzer-service.ts — imports GraphBuilder, DependencyGraph, GraphNode
  - src/analyzer/graph-analyzer.ts — imports DependencyGraph, GraphNode, GraphEdge
  - src/analyzer/graph-helpers.ts — imports DependencyGraph, GraphNode, GraphEdge
  - src/analyzer/index.ts — re-exports GraphBuilder, GraphNode, GraphEdge, DependencyGraph

Root test files:
  - __tests__/accuracy-validation.test.ts
  - __tests__/analyzer.test.ts
  - __tests__/graph-helpers.test.ts
  - __tests__/orphan-detection.test.ts
  - __tests__/phase5-integration.test.ts

These are the legacy analyzer infrastructure (AnalyzerService, GraphAnalyzer, graph-helpers) that predates the canonical pipeline. They are still exercised by root-level tests and used by CLI commands via analyzer-service.

Approach options (decide at create-workorder time):
(A) Full migration: refactor analyzer-service, graph-analyzer, graph-helpers to use ExportedGraph — requires architectural decision about deprecating the entire legacy analyzer path.
(B) Shim: replace graph-builder.ts with a minimal re-export stub so it still compiles, then migrate internal files incrementally.
(C) Test-only: migrate the 5 root __tests__/ files first, then assess whether the src/analyzer/ files can be consolidated.
